commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining decision is maintainer acceptance of the new public native-only inherit sentinel and ACP rejection contract, not a narrow automated repair.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain concern was found; the diff changes TypeScript runtime logic, tests, docs, and generated prompt snapshots without touching dependencies, workflows, secrets, or install scrip... (sha=83a671b64a45c73fc8406f064d58fd5339d7a9c3)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds native `sessions_spawn` model inheritance through `model: "inherit"`, threads requester model context into native subagent spawning, rejects explicit ACP `inherit`, and updates docs, prompt snapshots, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +238, Tests +427, Docs +21. Total +686 across 24 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR rather than a broken existing contract. Current main source and docs show no supported `model: "inherit"` path, and the PR body supplies after-change Gateway proof for the new behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining decision is maintainer acceptance of the new public native-only `inherit` sentinel and ACP rejection contract, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: feat(agents): inherit requester model for subagents
target.body:
## Summary

- Let native subagent spawns resolve `model: "inherit"` from the requester session's active model, including model context passed through `sessions_spawn` and persisted requester session state fallback.
- Normalize inherited provider/model refs so provider-qualified requester models whose model id also contains `/` are preserved, for example `vllm/openrouter/auto`.
- Preserve existing model precedence: explicit tool model, per-agent subagent model, default subagent model, target agent model, then global/runtime default.
- Keep model aliases authoritative, so a configured alias named `inherit` still selects that concrete model instead of the inheritance sentinel.
- Reject explicit `model: "inherit"` for `runtime: "acp"` instead of silently omitting the ACP model override; configured native `subagents.model: "inherit"` is intentionally not treated as an ACP model override.
- Update subagent docs/config docs/tool descriptions and focused regression coverage.
- Supersedes #87628 with a clean replacement branch based on latest `main`.

## Linked context

Closes N/A

Related #87628

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request known; this is a replacement for the previous contributor PR.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Native `sessions_spawn` can explicitly inherit the requester session's active provider-qualified model while preserving existing configured model precedence and alias handling. ACP `model: "inherit"` is now rejected explicitly.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS at current head `83a671b64a45c73fc8406f064d58fd5339d7a9c3`, using a temporary isolated Gateway and a local fake OpenAI-compatible `vllm` endpoint. The temporary model ref was `vllm/openrouter/auto` so the provider is qualified and the model id itself contains `/`, matching the slash-preservation case.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```console
$ OPENCLAW_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-89469-proof-client \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-89469-proof-client \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-89469-proof.ggFiDJ/openclaw.json \
  VLLM_API_KEY=proof-key OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=proof-token \
  pnpm openclaw gateway call sessions.patch --json --timeout 10000 --token proof-token \
  --params '{"key":"agent:main:main","agentId":"main","model":"vllm/openrouter/auto","label":"Proof parent"}'
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "entry": {
    "providerOverride": "vllm",
    "modelOverride": "openrouter/auto",
    "modelOverrideSource": "user"
  },
  "resolved": {
    "modelProvider": "vllm",
    "model": "openrouter/auto"
  }
}
```

Then invoke the real Gateway `tools.invoke` path for `sessions_spawn`:

```console
$ OPENCLAW_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-89469-proof-client \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-89469-proof-client \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-89469-proof.ggFiDJ/openclaw.json \
  VLLM_API_KEY=proof-key OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=proof-token \
  pnpm openclaw gateway call tools.invoke --json --timeout 60000 --token proof-token \
  --params '{"name":"sessions_spawn","sessionKey":"agent:main:main","idempotencyKey":"inherit-proof-2","args":{"task":"Reply exactly PROOF_OK after inheriting the requester model.","model":"inherit","cleanup":"keep"}}'
```

Observed `sessions_spawn` result:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "toolName": "sessions_spawn",
  "output": {
    "details": {
      "status": "accepted",
      "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:2d1d6872-85f3-43d2-bc85-c9edb970e51f",
      "runId": "61e78ed7-59a0-4bc5-b6ab-4f6cdb00a31f",
      "mode": "run",
      "resolvedModel": "vllm/openrouter/auto",
      "resolvedProvider": "vllm",
      "modelApplied": true
    }
  },
  "source": "core"
}
```

Child session store after the Gateway run:

```json
{
  "childKey": "agent:main:subagent:2d1d6872-85f3-43d2-bc85-c9edb970e51f",
  "child": {
    "model": "openrouter/auto",
    "modelOverride": "openrouter/auto",
    "modelOverrideSource": "user",
    "modelProvider": "vllm",
    "providerOverride": "vllm",
    "systemPromptReport": {
      "provider": "vllm",
      "model": "openrouter/auto"
    }
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: `sessions_spawn` returned an accepted child run with `resolvedModel: "vllm/openrouter/auto"` and the child session persisted both `modelProvider: "vllm"` and `modelOverride: "openrouter/auto"`.

What was not tested: Live external provider generation with real credentials was not used. The local fake provider was enough to exercise the Gateway, `tools.invoke`, `sessions_spawn`, session patching, and child session persistence paths without touching a real account.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --write`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`
- Earlier branch validation retained on this PR: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/live-model-dynamic-candidates.test.ts src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Model selection tests for explicit/configured `inherit`, requester model resolution, missing requester model, and `inherit` aliases.
- Native `spawnSubagentDirect` and `sessions_spawn` tool tests proving requester model context is forwarded and persisted.
- Provider-qualified nested model inheritance tests for live requester context and persisted requester fallback.
- ACP tests proving explicit `model: "inherit"` is rejected for ACP, aliases named `inherit` still resolve to concrete models, and configured native `subagents.model: "inherit"` does not become an ACP model override.
- Prompt/tool description/docs coverage was regenerated or updated where behavior changed.

What failed before this fix, if known?

ClawSweeper flagged that ACP `model: "inherit"` silently became `undefined` because ACP has no native requester-model inheritance context. This revision returns an explicit `runtime_policy` error for that ACP tool override instead.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A; focused regression tests and docs were added or updated.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Native subagent model selection can now explicitly inherit the requester session model. ACP now rejects explicit `model: "inherit"` instead of silently falling back to the ACP harness default.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. `agents.defaults.subagents.model` and `agents.list[].subagents.model` now document the `"inherit"` sentinel for native subagents while preserving aliases named `inherit`.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No direct security/auth/secret/network changes. Tool execution behavior changes only for subagent model selection when `inherit` is selected or when ACP receives an unsupported explicit inherit sentinel.

What is the highest-risk area?

Model precedence across native subagents, ACP runtime options, nested provider/model refs, and alias resolution.

How is that risk mitigated?

Focused tests cover explicit overrides, configured defaults, alias precedence, missing requester model errors, native persistence, provider-qualified nested model refs, ACP rejection, ACP alias compatibility, and sessions patch behavior. A temporary real Gateway proof also exercised `sessions.patch` plus `tools.invoke`/`sessions_spawn`.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

CI and maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Author-side changes and local proof are complete; CI/re-review is next.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed ClawSweeper's ACP `model: "inherit"` finding and added real Gateway `sessions_spawn` proof for provider-qualified nested model inheritance.
target.author: gausons

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: Gausons
commands.command: re-review — i believe this is a case where source-level proof is sufficient for a defensive null guard. the unit test demonstrates the race condition and the fix. no real behavior proof is practical for a timing-based null check
commands.freeform_prompt: — I believe this is a case where source-level proof is sufficient for a defensive null guard. The unit test demonstrates the race condition and the fix. No real behavior proof is practical for a timing-based null check.
